text.title: Tailwind Core Patterns: cn(), Variants, Responsive
text.summary: The cn() merge helper, variant object pattern, mobile-first responsive classes, and concentric CSS class ordering.

section.title: cn() Helper

rule.statement: Use cn() (clsx + tailwind-merge) for all conditional and merged class compositions. Never use string concatenation or template literals to build Tailwind class lists.
rule.rationale: tailwind-merge resolves Tailwind class conflicts (e.g. p-4 vs p-6) by keeping only the last applicable class. String concatenation produces duplicate conflicting classes that behave unpredictably depending on CSS specificity.

section.reference: cn() helper implementation using clsx and tailwind-merge

example.code:
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
<div className={cn( "base-styles rounded-lg p-4", isActive && "ring-2 ring-blue-500", isDisabled && "opacity-50 pointer-events-none", className // allow override from parent )} />

example.note: p-4 and p-6 both appear in the class string; the winner depends on CSS source order, not intent.

rule.statement: Define component variants as a plain object mapping variant name to class string, then select via cn(). Do not branch on variant with if/else chains in JSX.
rule.rationale: Object variants are type-safe when combined with TypeScript keyof, readable at a glance, and easy to extend without touching render logic.

rule.statement: Order Tailwind classes from outermost to innermost concern: layout/position → box model → borders → background → typography → effects. Use prettier-plugin-tailwindcss to enforce this automatically.
rule.rationale: Consistent ordering makes class strings scannable and diffable. Auto-sorting eliminates merge conflicts caused by developers adding classes in different positions.

example.code: <div className=" relative flex flex-col w-full max-w-md p-6 m-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg bg-white shadow-lg text-gray-900 text-sm font-medium transition-all duration-200 hover:shadow-xl " />

rule.statement: Always design mobile-first. Apply base classes for the smallest viewport and override with md:, lg:, xl: prefixes for larger screens. Never design desktop-first and try to shrink with sm: overrides.
rule.rationale: Mobile-first generates less CSS (default = mobile, overrides only for larger) and aligns with how Tailwind's breakpoints are defined (min-width, not max-width).

example.code: <div className=" flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:gap-8 lg:justify-between " />
